title: crotalaria humilis
intro: Crotalaria humilis is a low-growing, perennial shrub native to tropical Africa, commonly known as the 'dwarf rattlebox' due to its rattle-like seed pods. Traditionally, this plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly its roots and leaves, which contain alkaloids that exhibit anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities. The health benefits of Crotalaria humilis include relief from fever, cough, and respiratory issues, as well as its potential to treat skin conditions and wounds. In herbal preparations, the roots and leaves of Crotalaria humilis can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are often used in traditional African medicine to promote overall well-being.
